findings.description: generate_invite_code() uses Postgres random() which is a PRNG seeded from the system clock, not a CSPRNG. With 32^8 (~1.1 trillion) possible codes and knowledge of approximate creation time, an attacker could narrow the search space. Invite codes are the sole authentication factor for joining a group. Status: OPEN (unchanged from prior audit).
findings.recommendation: Replace floor(random() * length(charset)) with floor((get_byte(gen_random_bytes(1), 0)::integer / 256.0) * length(charset)) to use a cryptographically secure random source.

findings.description: The khatm_participants INSERT policy only checks auth.uid() IS NOT NULL. Any authenticated user can insert a participant row into any group_id, effectively joining any group without an invite code. The invite code validation in useJoinKhatm is client-side only. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Use a server-side RPC join_group(invite_code, name) that validates the invite code and inserts the row with SECURITY DEFINER. Remove the permissive INSERT policy.

findings.description: The khatm_groups INSERT policy only checks auth.uid() IS NOT NULL. A malicious user could create a group with another user's ID as admin_user_id. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Add WITH CHECK (admin_user_id = auth.uid()) to the khatm_groups INSERT policy.

findings.description: The Edge Function catch-all error handler returns String(e) in the response body via the 'detail' field, potentially exposing stack traces or database connection strings. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Return only JSON.stringify({ error: 'Internal server error' }). Remove the 'detail: String(e)' field.

findings.description: The notification-scheduler Edge Function accepts any HTTP request without authentication while using the SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. Any caller who discovers the URL can trigger mass notifications. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Add Authorization header verification with a shared secret known only to the Supabase cron scheduler.

findings.description: The admin/co-admin UPDATE policy on khatm_juz_assignments allows updating any column including group_id, participant_id, and juz_number. A compromised admin client can re-attribute assignments. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Add a BEFORE UPDATE trigger to prevent changes to group_id, participant_id, and juz_number after initial insertion.

findings.description: useCreateKhatm performs 4 sequential DB operations without a transaction. Partial failure leaves orphaned groups with no admin participant. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Wrap group creation in a create_khatm(input jsonb) SECURITY DEFINER RPC that performs all steps in a single transaction.

findings.description: The khatm_progress_updates INSERT policy does not allow admin override writes where participant_id differs from auth.uid(). Admin override writes fail silently. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Extend the INSERT policy to also allow is_admin_or_coadmin() for the assignment's group.

findings.description: claim_juz RPC uses COUNT-then-INSERT. Two concurrent callers can both pass the count check before either inserts, exceeding max_per_juz. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Add PERFORM pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(p_group_id::text || p_juz_number::text)) before the COUNT check.

findings.description: Both claim_juz and start_new_cycle SECURITY DEFINER RPCs perform security-sensitive operations but neither writes to khatm_audit_log. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Add audit log INSERT statements at the end of each RPC with the appropriate action_type.

findings.description: handleArchiveGroup in GroupSettingsBottomSheet and CompletionScreen directly calls supabase.from('khatm_groups').update() bypassing the mutation layer and audit logging. Archive actions have no audit trail. Status: OPEN (unchanged).
findings.recommendation: Route archive operations through a useArchiveGroup mutation that includes audit logging with action_type 'GROUP_ARCHIVED'.

findings.description: NEW: supabase/migrations/004_push_token.sql adds a push_token TEXT column to khatm_participants with no column-level restrictions. The existing SELECT policy exposes push tokens to all JOINED group members. Push tokens are device-identifying PII.
findings.recommendation: Move push_token to a separate push_tokens table with a SELECT policy restricting reads to service role or the token owner only.

findings.description: NEW: useUpdateGroupSettings logs the regenerated invite code plaintext in khatm_audit_log (new_value: { invite_code: newCode }). All JOINED group members can read this, including recently removed members before full removal.
findings.recommendation: Log action_type 'INVITE_CODE_REGENERATED' without including the new code value in new_value.

findings.description: NEW: The .gitignore file does not explicitly list .env files, increasing risk of accidentally committing secrets.
